---
title: "pwmconfig does not always start (on boot/resume)"
date: 2010-01-24
forum: Hardware
---

### Post by Mr_Pag on 2010-01-24
Hi there, I am running pwmconfig on Karmic 9.10, all sensors were detected OK and the CPU runs within the defined spec, topping out at about 35 degrees Celsius under load - therefore the fanspeed is normally minimum (around 1140 RPM):

```

# Configuration file generated by pwmconfig, changes will be lost
INTERVAL=10
FCTEMPS=hwmon1/device/pwm2=hwmon1/device/temp2_input
FCFANS= hwmon1/device/pwm2=hwmon1/device/fan2_input
MINTEMP=hwmon1/device/pwm2=30
MAXTEMP=hwmon1/device/pwm2=60
MINSTART=hwmon1/device/pwm2=20
MINSTOP=hwmon1/device/pwm2=20
MINPWM=hwmon1/device/pwm2=20
MAXPWM=hwmon1/device/pwm2=240

```

However I have two issues with pwmconfig:

* It does not always start on boot, setting the fan to a constant and noisy ~1900 RPM.

* Resume from suspend also sets the fanspeed to this value, pwmconfig does not reinitialise?

Can anyone help me resolve these issues, my mobo is an Asrock 939N68PV-GLAN? Thanks in advance!

Cheers,

Mr_Pag
